courses.name: Fundamentals of Computer Science
learningObjectives: Understand basic programming concepts (variables, loops, conditionals)
Grasp algorithm design and analysis
Apply computational thinking to biomedical problems
courses.references: Cormen et al., Silberschatz
exampleQuestions: Explain the difference between an algorithm and a program, and provide an example from biomedical engineering.
What is the time complexity of binary search, and why is it important for large medical datasets?

courses.name: Mathematics
learningObjectives: Master calculus fundamentals (derivatives, integrals)
Understand linear algebra and matrix operations
Apply mathematical concepts to biomedical systems
courses.references: Standard calculus and linear algebra textbooks
exampleQuestions: Define the derivative and explain its physical meaning in the context of biomedical signals.
Show how to solve a system of linear equations and relate it to circuit analysis.

courses.name: Chemistry
learningObjectives: Understand atomic structure and chemical bonding
Master organic and inorganic reaction mechanisms
Apply chemistry to biomedical contexts (biochemistry)
courses.references: Standard chemistry textbooks (organic, inorganic, biochemistry)
exampleQuestions: Explain peptide bonding and its role in protein structure.
What is an enzyme, and how does it catalyze biochemical reactions?

courses.name: General Physics
learningObjectives: Master mechanics (kinematics, dynamics, energy)
Understand waves, oscillations, and sound
Apply physics principles to biomedical devices
courses.references: Halliday/Resnick/Walker, Serway
exampleQuestions: Explain Newton's second law and how it applies to muscle contraction.
Describe the properties of ultrasound waves used in medical imaging.

courses.name: Economics and Management
learningObjectives: Understand healthcare economics and cost-benefit analysis
Apply project management to biomedical device development
Grasp regulatory and business frameworks for medical devices
courses.references: Healthcare economics and medical device management texts
exampleQuestions: What are the main regulatory frameworks (FDA, CE Mark) for medical device approval?
How would you conduct a cost-benefit analysis for a new diagnostic device?

courses.name: General English/Italian
learningObjectives: Develop conversational Italian skills for biomedical contexts
Build professional communication vocabulary
Practice presenting scientific concepts in Italian
courses.references: UCBM language and professional communication guidelines
exampleQuestions: Parla di te: qual è il tuo nome, dove vieni, e perché studi ingegneria biomedica?
Descrivi i tuoi interessi e i tuoi obiettivi nel campo della bioingegneria.

courses.name: Physiology
learningObjectives: Understand organ system functions and homeostasis
Master physiological mechanisms (cardiovascular, respiratory, nervous systems)
Connect physiology to biomedical device design
courses.references: Guyton & Hall, Costanzo Physiology
exampleQuestions: Explain the cardiac action potential and its role in heartbeat generation.
Describe the regulation of blood pressure and how antihypertensive drugs work.

courses.name: Anatomy
learningObjectives: Understand human body structure and organization
Master anatomical terminology and relationships
Connect anatomy to physiological function and device design
courses.references: Gray's Anatomy for Students, Netter's Atlas
exampleQuestions: Describe the anatomy of the heart, including chambers, valves, and major vessels.
Explain the location and function of the major bones in the human skeleton.

courses.name: Advanced Physics
learningObjectives: Master quantum mechanics and wave-particle duality
Understand optics and applications in medical imaging
Apply modern physics to diagnostic technologies
courses.references: Advanced physics texts, biomedical imaging applications
exampleQuestions: Explain the photoelectric effect and its application in medical imaging detectors.
Describe how X-ray generation involves quantum transitions.

courses.name: Mathematics II
learningObjectives: Master differential equations and their solutions
Understand vector calculus and multivariable analysis
Apply transforms (Fourier, Laplace) to biomedical signals
courses.references: Advanced calculus, differential equations, vector calculus
exampleQuestions: Solve the differential equation for RC circuit charging and explain its biological analogue.
What is the Fourier transform and how is it used in biomedical signal analysis?

courses.name: Probability and Statistics
learningObjectives: Master probability distributions and their applications
Understand hypothesis testing and confidence intervals
Apply statistical methods to biomedical research and clinical trials
courses.references: Standard statistics and biomedical data analysis texts
exampleQuestions: What is a confidence interval, and how would you interpret a 95% CI for a drug efficacy study?
Explain Type I and Type II errors in the context of a medical diagnostic test.

courses.name: Healthcare Information Systems and Telemedicine
learningObjectives: Understand EHR systems and healthcare IT architecture
Master telemedicine platforms and remote patient monitoring
Apply IT standards (HL7, DICOM) in clinical settings
courses.references: EHR systems, telemedicine platforms, healthcare IT standards
exampleQuestions: What is HL7, and how does it enable interoperability between hospital systems?
Describe the security and privacy requirements for patient data in HIPAA compliance.

courses.name: Electronics and Electrotechnics
learningObjectives: Master circuit analysis and Kirchhoff's laws
Understand electronic components (resistors, capacitors, transistors)
Apply circuits to biomedical instrumentation design
courses.references: Circuit theory, electronics textbooks, biomedical instrumentation
exampleQuestions: Analyze a simple RC circuit and derive its transfer function.
Explain how an operational amplifier (op-amp) is used in biomedical sensor conditioning.

courses.name: Mechanics of Solids
learningObjectives: Master stress, strain, and material properties
Understand deformation and elasticity in biological tissues
Apply mechanics to implant design and tissue engineering
courses.references: Mechanics and materials science textbooks
exampleQuestions: Define stress and strain, and explain Young's modulus in the context of bone mechanics.
How does material failure occur in orthopedic implants?

courses.name: Transport Phenomena and Thermodynamics
learningObjectives: Master heat and mass transport phenomena
Understand thermodynamic principles in biological systems
Apply transport models to drug delivery and perfusion
courses.references: Transport phenomena and thermodynamics textbooks, biological applications
exampleQuestions: Derive the heat diffusion equation and explain its application to thermal therapy.
Describe mass transport in biological membranes and its role in drug delivery.

courses.name: Technical English/Italian
learningObjectives: Master technical vocabulary in biomedical engineering Italian
Present research and technical concepts in Italian
Develop professional communication for international collaboration
courses.references: UCBM technical language and professional communication guidelines
exampleQuestions: Spiega un articolo di ricerca biomedica in italiano: cos'è la ricerca, quali sono i metodi e i risultati?
Descrivi un dispositivo medico (ad esempio, un pacemaker) e il suo funzionamento in italiano.

courses.name: Biomedical Signal Processing
learningObjectives: Master signal acquisition and digitization
Understand filtering and spectral analysis
Apply signal processing to ECG, EEG, and EMG analysis
courses.references: Signal processing textbooks, biomedical signal analysis
exampleQuestions: Explain the Nyquist theorem and its importance in ECG sampling.
Design a low-pass filter to remove 60 Hz noise from a biomedical signal.

courses.name: Fundamental of Automatic Control
learningObjectives: Master feedback control systems and stability analysis
Understand PID control and tuning
Apply control theory to biomedical devices (pacemakers, infusion pumps)
courses.references: Control theory textbooks, feedback systems
exampleQuestions: Explain what makes a control system stable, and apply the Routh-Hurwitz criterion.
Design a PID controller for insulin delivery in an artificial pancreas.

courses.name: Biomechanics
learningObjectives: Master kinematics and kinetics of human movement
Understand tissue mechanics and viscoelasticity
Apply biomechanics to rehabilitation and prosthetics design
courses.references: Ozkaya/Knudson Biomechanics, Y.C. Fung
exampleQuestions: Analyze the gait cycle using biomechanical principles and identify key joint angles.
Explain the viscoelastic properties of biological tissues and their measurement.

courses.name: Fundamentals of Bioengineering
learningObjectives: Integrate all bioengineering disciplines (anatomy, physics, electronics, materials)
Understand biomedical device design and development process
Apply systems thinking to complex biomedical problems
courses.references: Enderle & Bronzino, biomedical engineering principles
exampleQuestions: Design a simple ECG monitoring system: describe the electrode, signal conditioning, and analysis stages.
Walk through the development process of a new medical device, from concept to market.

courses.name: Measurements and Instrumentation in Biomedical Engineering and Standards for Medical Devices
learningObjectives: Master measurement principles and sensor technology
Understand biomedical device standards (ISO/IEC)
Apply regulatory requirements (FDA, CE Mark) in device development
courses.references: Instrumentation texts, ISO/IEC medical device standards
exampleQuestions: Explain measurement uncertainty and how it is propagated through a measurement chain.
What are the key requirements of IEC 60601 for electrical safety in medical devices?

courses.name: Humanities for Bioengineering
learningObjectives: Understand ethics in medical device design and use
Grasp the social, economic, and healthcare system context
Develop critical thinking on bioengineering's societal impact
courses.references: Ethics, professional responsibility, healthcare innovation
exampleQuestions: Discuss the ethical considerations in developing an AI diagnostic tool that could replace human radiologists.
How should biomedical engineers balance innovation with patient safety and cost considerations?
